This trend is accelerating, meaning profitability is moving further out of reach until commercial product revenue materializes. 🟢 𝐈𝐧𝐝𝐢𝐮𝐦 𝐏𝐡𝐨𝐬𝐩𝐡𝐢𝐝𝐞 𝐒𝐮𝐩𝐩𝐥𝐲 𝐂𝐡𝐚𝐢𝐧 𝐆𝐚𝐩 The company's core thesis remains intact: the massive AI data center buildout requires advanced photonics, and the industry is constrained by Indium Phosphide availability. Customers are actively engaging Aeluma to bridge near-term supply gaps, validating the core technology platform. 🟢 𝐏𝐚𝐭𝐡 𝐭𝐨 𝐕𝐨𝐥𝐮𝐦𝐞 𝐌𝐚𝐧𝐮𝐟𝐚𝐜𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐂𝐥𝐞𝐚𝐫𝐞𝐝 [NEW] Transitioning from R&D to commercial scale requires heavy-hitting foundry partners. The formal addition of Tower Semiconductor and Sumitomo Chemical Advanced Technology to Aeluma's supply chain is a major milestone. It proves to prospective commercial customers that Aeluma can deliver in volume. 𝐎𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐫 𝐊𝐏𝐈𝐬 𝐓𝐨𝐭𝐚𝐥 𝐂𝐚𝐬𝐡 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐄𝐪𝐮𝐢𝐯𝐚𝐥𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐬: $37.8 million Stable. Down slightly from $38.6M at the end of December 2025. Following their successful public offering earlier in the fiscal year, Aeluma has secured a massive liquidity runway. This cash buffer is the company's strongest asset, allowing them to absorb the current operating losses while waiting for commercial contracts to ramp. 𝐀𝐝𝐣𝐮𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐝 𝐄𝐁𝐈𝐓𝐃𝐀: -$0.91 million Reversing. A year ago, the company posted a slight positive EBITDA of $109k. The recent quarters represent a structural reversal into deeper losses (-$917k in Q2, -$911k in Q3) as the company prioritizes team expansion and manufacturing readiness over near-term profitability. 𝐆𝐮𝐢𝐝𝐚𝐧𝐜𝐞 𝐅𝐘𝟐𝟔 𝐑𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐧𝐮𝐞: $4.2 - $4.6 million Decelerating significantly. The company slashed the top end of its prior guidance ($4.0 - $6.0 million) by $1.4 million. Given the $3.88M already recognized year-to-date, this target implies an average Q4 midpoint revenue of just $0.52 million. Management attributes the shortfall to delayed project start dates caused by government shutdowns. 𝐊𝐞𝐲 𝐐𝐮𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬 𝐕𝐢𝐬𝐢𝐛𝐢𝐥𝐢𝐭𝐲 𝐢𝐧𝐭𝐨 𝐅𝐘𝟐𝟕 With the start of several government contracts pushed out, how much of the $1.4 million removed from the top end of FY26 guidance will directly roll into Q1 or Q2 of FY27? 𝐂𝐨𝐦𝐦𝐞𝐫𝐜𝐢𝐚𝐥 𝐑𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐧𝐮𝐞 𝐓𝐢𝐦𝐞𝐥𝐢𝐧𝐞 The company highlighted $5 million in new development engagements. Are any of these engagements expected to transition from R&D to commercial product orders within the next 12 months? 𝐂𝐚𝐬𝐡 𝐁𝐮𝐫𝐧 𝐏𝐥𝐚𝐭𝐞𝐚𝐮 With operating expenses jumping to $3.3 million this quarter, have we reached a stabilized run-rate for OpEx, or should investors expect continued aggressive headcount expansion through the rest of the calendar year?"}}}, "rest_id": "2054672403662352565", "source": "Auto articles", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "2475", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

A revenue leap from $39,000 to $3.7 million highlights a bold expansion strategy at QCi, driven by strategic acquisitions. By acquiring a photonics manufacturer and a quantum communications firm, the company is building a vertically integrated photonics stack spanning component fabrication, quantum-secure networking, and optimization hardware. This is a calculated bet that controlling the supply chain from chip to system will become essential as photonic technologies scale.

Key insights from the recent report show clear forward momentum. The $110 million Luminar Semiconductor acquisition secures in-house capabilities for lasers, detectors, and advanced packaging built around thin-film lithium niobate, a material gaining serious traction for high-speed optical applications. Additionally, the $5 million NuCrypt deal integrates vital quantum communications technology. Operating expenses reached $19.8 million alongside a $4.1 million net loss, reflecting a period of intensive scaling and integration. However, with roughly $1.4 billion in cash and a $16 million contract backlog, the balance sheet provides ample runway to absorb development costs and expand manufacturing.

The deployment of a Dirac-3 optimization machine on the Quantum Corridor network is a compelling milestone. Making quantum optimization remotely accessible through a quantum-safe communications infrastructure is exactly the kind of practical integration the industry requires.

Photonics currently sits at a fascinating intersection. It is commercially mature in telecom and datacenter infrastructure, yet holds long-term promise as a foundational building block for quantum systems. Positioning across both markets ensures that today's photonic expertise translates into a future quantum advantage.

The next phase will demonstrate how these integrated capabilities drive sustained growth, and whether small-batch chip prototyping at Fab 1 can evolve into full production scale at a future Fab 2 #QuantumComputing

text:
BAM
$TSEM — the only foundry on Earth with a production-grade silicon photonics PDK, ~80% SiPho market share, and NVDA, Broadcom, Marvell, Coherent and Lumentum all depend on them. $920M capex funded 70%+ by customer prepayments takes capacity 5x by Q4 2026. SiPho revenue already up 115% YoY — they're the picks-and-shovels for every AI optical interconnect.

@tommyleed8888 I wasn't aware

If I already thought it would do it under normal conditions, now even more so

But this is almost certainly due to a widespread rally across the entire photonics sector

And especially a short term trend reversal in $SIVE

text:
The AI infrastructure trade is becoming much broader than just GPUs.  

A growing part of the market is now focused on:
memory, optics, photonics, networking, packaging and next-gen materials.

Some smaller/mid-cap names getting increasing attention:

$LITE – optical networking, lasers and datacenter photonics. One of the more direct AI optics plays.

$COHR – major photonics/laser company with exposure to InP, AI interconnects and advanced optical infrastructure.

$AAOI – optical transceivers/connectivity for hyperscale datacenters and AI clusters.

$CRDO – high-speed connectivity and retimers critical for 800G / 1.6T AI networking.

$POET – highly speculative optical engine/photonics integration story targeting AI interconnect bottlenecks.

$ALMU – tiny microcap focused on compound semiconductors, InP/GaAs and photonics-related materials.

$AXTI – upstream supplier of InP/GaAs substrates used in optics, RF and advanced communications.

$MRAM – speculative next-generation memory technology (MRAM) with potential low-latency / persistent memory applications.

$VLN – high-speed connectivity chips, currently more automotive/industrial focused but tied to the broader data connectivity theme.

The key idea:
AI is evolving from a software story into a full industrial infrastructure buildout.

Compute alone is no longer enough.  
Bandwidth, memory, optics, cooling and power are becoming equally critical.

text:
Across Silicon Photonics and CPO (co-packaged optics, fiber built into the chip), $POET is doing the heaviest lifting.

The AI rack is mid-transition from copper to light.

$NVDA's Rubin is designed around it.

$TSEM printed SiPh revenue +70% YoY and is dropping $650M to triple capacity by mid-2026.

$POET at $1.7Bn cap is small next to that wave. +80% in 30 days tells you the tape noticed.

The IF: optical engines have to land in volume design wins, not stay in pilots.
